steps.title: Use one of correctly
steps.content: The phrase one of requires a singular possessive author name followed by a plural noun: one novel from among Austen's novels. The comma belongs after novels, before the appositive title Pride and Prejudice.

steps.title: Reject possessive errors
steps.content: Choice B makes both Austen and novels plural possessives. Choice C omits the possessive form. Choice D makes novel singular and possessive, which would mean something belonging to one novel rather than one novel among several. The answer is A.
